subtitle: Tension and stiffness from posture and arm swing

strava_risk_triggers.risk_note: Trap tension builds on long runs over 90 minutes

mild.description: Tightness or stiffness after long runs, eases with movement
mild.can_run_note: Yes — focus on relaxing shoulders during the run
why: Forward head posture while running — head drifting ahead of the spine
Tight chest and weak upper back from prolonged sitting
Arm swing tension radiating upward into the trapezius
relief: Chin tucks: gently retract chin, hold 5 seconds × 10 reps
Upper trap stretch: tilt ear to shoulder, hold 30s each side
Heat pad on upper back and neck for 15 min post-run
Self-massage with a tennis ball against the wall
prevention: Run with gaze on the horizon, not down at your feet
Relax shoulders every km — drop them away from your ears
Band pull-aparts and face pulls 3× per week

moderate.description: Persistent stiffness, limited rotation, headache at base of skull
moderate.can_run_note: Short easy runs only — stop if headache worsens
why: Chronic forward head posture creating muscle imbalance
Upper cervical joint stiffness from sustained poor posture
Levator scapulae and SCM overactivation compensating for weak deep neck flexors
relief: Gentle neck rotations in warm water or shower
Suboccipital release: lie on two tennis balls at base of skull for 5 min
Reduce screen time and check desk ergonomics
prevention: Deep neck flexor strengthening: chin tuck holds 10s × 10 daily
Check running form on video — head position
Strengthen mid and lower trapezius with Y-T-W exercises
Sleep with a supportive pillow — avoid sleeping on stomach

severe.description: Sharp pain, numbness or tingling into the arm, severe headache
severe.can_run_note: Do not run — possible cervical nerve involvement
why: Possible cervical disc irritation or nerve root compression
Significant joint restriction requiring manual therapy
Referred pain pattern needing proper diagnosis
relief: Complete rest from running and overhead activity
See a physiotherapist or GP urgently if arm symptoms present
Gentle heat and pain relief medication as directed
Avoid positions that increase arm or hand tingling
prevention: Do not return to running until neck rotation is pain-free
Cervical rehabilitation programme under physio guidance
Imaging if neurological symptoms persist beyond 1 week

see_physio_if: Numbness or tingling into the arm or hand
Severe headache at the base of the skull
No improvement after 1 week of self-management
